recipes.title: Lahmacun
recipes.description: Thin Turkish flatbread topped with spiced minced lamb, tomatoes, peppers, and parsley, baked until crisp and rolled with lemon.
steps: Mix flour, yeast, salt, and warm water into a soft dough; knead 8 minutes and rest covered for 1 hour.
Pulse tomato, pepper, onion, garlic, and parsley in a food processor until finely chopped, then drain excess liquid.
Combine the chopped vegetables with minced lamb, tomato paste, pepper paste, cumin, paprika, and salt into a spreadable topping.
Divide the dough into 8 balls and roll each one paper-thin on a floured surface.
Spread a thin layer of the lamb mixture across each round, pressing it right to the edges.
Bake on a preheated stone or steel at 260C for 4 to 6 minutes until the edges crisp and the topping cooks through.
Squeeze fresh lemon over the top, scatter parsley, and roll up to serve hot.

recipes.title: Iskender Kebab
recipes.description: Sliced döner lamb served over cubes of pide bread, drenched in tomato sauce and browned butter with a side of yogurt.
steps: Slice the lamb thinly and marinate with grated garlic, olive oil, paprika, salt, and pepper for at least 1 hour.
Sear the lamb slices in a hot pan for 2 to 3 minutes per side until just cooked, then rest.
Cut pide bread into bite-size cubes and toast lightly in a dry skillet.
Warm the tomato passata with a pinch of salt until heated through.
Melt the butter in a small saucepan until foaming and lightly browned.
Layer toasted bread on plates, top with sliced lamb, then pour tomato sauce and browned butter over everything.
Serve immediately with a generous spoonful of cold yogurt on the side.

recipes.title: Manti
recipes.description: Tiny Turkish dumplings filled with spiced beef, boiled and topped with garlic yogurt, melted butter, and red pepper oil.
steps: Make a stiff dough from flour, eggs, water, and salt; knead 8 minutes and rest 30 minutes wrapped.
Grate onion and squeeze out the juice, then mix with minced beef, salt, and pepper.
Roll the dough paper-thin and cut into 2 cm squares.
Place a tiny pea-size portion of filling on each square, then pinch the four corners up to seal into a parcel.
Boil the manti in salted water for 8 to 10 minutes until the pasta is tender and the meat is cooked.
Whisk yogurt with grated garlic and a pinch of salt to make the sauce.
Brown butter with red pepper flakes in a small pan until aromatic.
Plate the dumplings, spoon over garlic yogurt, drizzle with red pepper butter, and dust with dried mint.

recipes.title: Pide
recipes.description: Boat-shaped Turkish flatbread filled with spiced minced meat, peppers, and tomato, baked until golden and bubbling.
steps: Combine flour, yeast, salt, oil, and warm water; knead into a soft dough and rise 1 hour.
Mix minced lamb with finely chopped onion, tomato, pepper, tomato paste, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Divide dough into 4 pieces and roll each into a long oval shape.
Spread the meat filling down the center, then fold the long edges up and pinch the ends into a boat shape.
Brush the dough edges with egg yolk thinned with water.
Bake on a hot stone at 240C for 12 to 15 minutes until the dough is golden and the filling is sizzling.
Slice across into pieces and serve hot with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Köfte
recipes.description: Hand-shaped Turkish meatballs of seasoned minced lamb and beef, grilled until charred outside and juicy within.
steps: Grate the onion and squeeze out the liquid through a fine cloth.
Combine both meats with onion, garlic, breadcrumbs, egg, parsley, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Knead the mixture firmly for 5 minutes until tacky, then refrigerate 30 minutes.
Shape into oval patties about 8 cm long with damp hands.
Grill over hot charcoal or in a ridged pan for 4 minutes per side until well charred.
Rest the köfte 2 minutes, then serve with grilled peppers, onions, and warm flatbread.

recipes.title: Dolma
recipes.description: Grape leaves stuffed with seasoned rice, herbs, and pine nuts, simmered slowly in olive oil and lemon.
steps: Rinse grape leaves and blanch briefly in hot water to remove brine.
Sauté finely chopped onion in olive oil until soft, then add pine nuts and toast lightly.
Stir in rinsed rice, currants, cinnamon, allspice, and salt; cook 2 minutes.
Add 200 ml water and simmer until just absorbed, then fold in chopped dill and mint.
Place a leaf vein-side up, spoon a teaspoon of filling near the stem, fold sides in, and roll tightly.
Pack the rolls seam-side down in a heavy pot, layered with extra leaves on the bottom.
Pour over the juice of 2 lemons, remaining olive oil, and enough water to barely cover.
Weight with a plate and simmer covered for 45 minutes; cool to room temperature before serving.

recipes.title: Imam Bayildi
recipes.description: Whole eggplants stuffed with onion, garlic, and tomato, slow-braised in olive oil until silky and sweet.
steps: Peel eggplants in stripes and salt them; rest 30 minutes, then rinse and pat dry.
Fry the eggplants in olive oil on all sides until softened, then transfer to a baking dish.
Slice each eggplant lengthwise to create a deep pocket without cutting through.
Sauté sliced onions in the same oil until golden, then add chopped tomatoes, garlic, and parsley.
Season the filling with salt, sugar, and lemon juice and cook until the tomatoes break down.
Stuff each eggplant generously with the onion-tomato mixture.
Add 100 ml water to the dish, drizzle with extra olive oil, cover, and bake at 180C for 45 minutes.
Cool to room temperature and serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Menemen
recipes.description: Soft scrambled eggs cooked into a skillet of tomatoes, peppers, and Turkish chili, served straight from the pan.
steps: Soften finely chopped onion and green pepper in olive oil over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Add grated tomatoes, salt, and pepper flakes, then simmer until thickened, about 8 minutes.
Crack eggs directly into the pan and stir slowly with a spatula until just set but still glossy.
Crumble feta over the top and finish with cracked black pepper.
Serve immediately in the skillet with warm crusty bread for scooping.

recipes.title: Kuzu Tandır
recipes.description: Slow-roasted Turkish lamb shoulder cooked until fork-tender, traditionally served on flatbread with rice pilaf.
steps: Score the lamb shoulder and rub all over with cumin, paprika, salt, pepper, garlic, and olive oil.
Sit the lamb on sliced onions and bay leaves in a deep roasting tray, cover tightly with foil.
Pour 200 ml water into the base of the tray to create steam.
Roast at 150C for 4 hours, then uncover and roast another 30 minutes to brown the surface.
Brush with melted butter during the final 10 minutes for a glossy finish.
Rest the lamb 15 minutes, then pull apart with two forks.
Serve over Turkish flatbread with the rendered juices spooned over.

recipes.title: Sucuk
recipes.description: Spiced Turkish dry-cured beef sausage pan-fried until crisp and served with eggs, bread, or grilled tomato.
steps: Slice sucuk on the diagonal into 5 mm rounds.
Heat a dry skillet and lay the sucuk slices flat; cook 2 minutes per side until crisp and the fat renders.
Add halved tomatoes cut-side down and cook 2 minutes until softened.
Crack eggs directly into the rendered sucuk fat and cook to taste.
Sprinkle with red pepper flakes and serve at once with toasted Turkish bread.

recipes.title: Künefe
recipes.description: Crispy shredded kataifi pastry layered with stretchy unsalted cheese, baked golden, and soaked in rosewater syrup.
steps: Boil sugar, water, and lemon juice for 8 minutes, then stir in rosewater and chill the syrup.
Pull apart the kataifi and toss thoroughly with melted butter until every strand is coated.
Press half the buttered pastry into a round shallow pan to form a tight base.
Lay sliced mozzarella over the base, leaving a 1 cm border, then top with the remaining pastry, pressing down.
Cook over medium heat for 8 minutes until the bottom is deeply golden, then flip and cook the second side.
Pour the cold syrup over the hot künefe immediately so it sizzles and absorbs.
Top generously with crushed pistachios and serve hot while the cheese is still stretchy.

recipes.title: Baklava
recipes.description: Layered phyllo pastry packed with chopped pistachios and walnuts, baked crisp and saturated with lemon syrup.
steps: Boil sugar, water, and lemon juice for 10 minutes to make a thin syrup, then cool completely.
Pulse pistachios and walnuts to a coarse crumb and mix with cinnamon.
Brush a 30 cm tray with melted butter and lay 8 sheets of phyllo, brushing each with butter.
Sprinkle a thin layer of nuts, then top with 2 buttered phyllo sheets; repeat the nut and pastry layers.
Finish with 8 buttered phyllo sheets on top and pour the remaining butter over the surface.
Cut into diamonds with a sharp knife all the way through to the bottom.
Bake at 170C for 50 minutes until uniformly deep golden.
Pour the cold syrup over the hot baklava, then rest for 4 hours so the layers absorb.

recipes.title: Lokum
recipes.description: Classic Turkish delight set from sugar syrup thickened with cornstarch, perfumed with rosewater and dusted with powdered sugar.
steps: Boil sugar, lemon juice, and 200 ml water to 115C, then remove from heat.
Whisk cornstarch with the remaining water until smooth, then bring to a low boil and cook to a thick paste.
Slowly stream the hot sugar syrup into the cornstarch paste, whisking constantly.
Cook the mixture over low heat for 50 minutes, stirring often, until deep golden and very thick.
Stir in rosewater and chopped pistachios off the heat.
Pour into an oiled and powdered-sugared 20 cm tray and chill overnight to set firm.
Cut into cubes and toss with powdered sugar to coat all sides.

recipes.title: Ayran
recipes.description: Salty, frothy Turkish yogurt drink whisked with cold water, the classic accompaniment to grilled kebabs.
steps: Whisk yogurt and salt in a bowl until smooth.
Slowly add cold water while whisking briskly to develop a thick layer of foam.
Pour over ice into tall glasses and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Çiğ Köfte
recipes.description: Vegan Turkish bulgur balls kneaded with tomato paste, spices, and fresh herbs, served with lettuce leaves and lemon.
steps: Pour 250 ml hot water over fine bulgur and let it absorb for 15 minutes.
Grate onion and garlic to a paste and mix into the bulgur with both pastes, cumin, pepper flakes, and salt.
Knead the mixture firmly with damp hands for 10 minutes until very sticky and uniform.
Fold in finely chopped parsley, spring onions, and olive oil.
Squeeze a small handful in your fist to form an elongated finger-shape köfte and place on a plate.
Repeat until all the mixture is shaped, then chill 30 minutes.
Serve with lemon wedges and crisp lettuce leaves for wrapping.

recipes.title: Şakşuka
recipes.description: Turkish meze of fried eggplant, courgette, and peppers smothered in a garlicky tomato sauce, served cold.
steps: Cube eggplant and courgette and salt them; rest 30 minutes, then pat dry.
Fry the eggplant in olive oil until golden, then drain on paper towels.
Repeat with the courgette and roughly chopped peppers.
In the remaining oil, sauté grated garlic for 30 seconds, then add chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, and pepper flakes.
Simmer the sauce 10 minutes until thickened.
Combine the fried vegetables on a serving plate and pour the warm sauce over the top.
Cool to room temperature and serve as a meze with bread.
